headline: E-wallet balances carry no PIDM deposit insurance as adoption hits 18.4 billion transactions
context: BNM's 2025 Annual Report shows e-payment transactions grew 25% to 18.4 billion in 2025. PIDM deposit insurance does not extend to e-wallet balances. Online fraud losses hit RM2.77 billion, the highest in three years — and FMOS resolved only 22% of e-wallet complaints in the consumer's favour.

cards.big: Malaysians made 18.4 billion e-payment transactions in 2025 — funds inside e-wallets carry no PIDM deposit insurance at all
cards.sub: BNM confirms e-payment transactions grew 25% to 18.4 billion. Government subsidies worth RM2 billion onboarded millions since 2020. Online fraud hit RM2.77 billion in 2025, the highest in three years.

cards.big: PIDM covers bank deposits up to RM250,000. E-wallet balances get none of it — trust account protections remain untested in Malaysian insolvency court.
cards.sub: BNM's 2025 e-money policy requires trust accounts rooted in Re Kayford common law, but these have not been stress-tested. Vcash's 2019 shutdown used manual refund forms.

cards.big: FMOS received 1,843 e-wallet complaints in 2025 — only 22% resolved in the consumer's favour, averaging 89 days against a 45-day target.
cards.sub: Of RM2.77B lost to online fraud, the scam pipeline recovered RM34M and returned RM6.7M to victims. Credit cards offer chargeback protection; e-wallets do not.

cards.big: BNM data: 63% of e-wallet accounts hold less than RM50 — trivial for T20 but critical for B40. Consumer Credit Act 2025 excludes e-wallet credit features.
cards.sub: eMADANI's RM1 billion went to STR recipients and those earning under RM100K. Women managing household micro-payments face targeted scam exposure.

cards.big: Every coalition — PH, PN, BN — rushed e-wallet subsidies for voter wins before protections were built. The political incentive has not changed.
Think of it like issuing driving licences to millions before building guardrails — and counting the pile-ups as an unlucky coincidence
cards.sub: Bank customers have deposit insurance, chargebacks, and decades of tested supervision. E-wallet users have trust accounts untested in Malaysian insolvency and a dispute service where 78% of their complaints fail.

cards.big: Three fixes would close the gap: tiered deposit protection for e-wallet balances, capital requirements scaling with operator float, and mandatory literacy in subsidies.
